steps.title: Use corresponding sides
steps.content: Similar rectangles have proportional corresponding side lengths. Match $AB$ with $QR$ and $AD$ with $QT$, then use the known corresponding side pair to set the scale factor.

steps.title: Identify corresponding sides and the scale factor
steps.content:
Since vertices A, B, C, and D correspond to vertices Q, R, S, and T respectively, side $AB$ (the height, 12 in) corresponds to side $QR$ (the height, 5 in). Both vertical sides are given, so we can find the scale factor:
$$k = \frac{AB}{QR} = \frac{12}{5}$$

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to find AD
steps.content:
Since the rectangles are similar, corresponding sides are proportional. Side $AD$ corresponds to side $QT$ (the bases), so:
$$\frac{AD}{QT} = \frac{12}{5}$$
Substitute $QT = 10$:
$$\frac{AD}{10} = \frac{12}{5}$$
$$AD = 10 \cdot \frac{12}{5} = \frac{120}{5} = 24 \text{ in}$$
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
